passes.diff_range: uncommitted working tree (detached HEAD at e11187e)

findings.title: Revert of committed fix reintroduces fragile autoescape config, risking silent XSS on future Starlette upgrade
findings.problem: This diff reverts commit e11187e ('fix: use preconfigured jinja2.Environment for autoescape'), which replaced Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True) with an explicitly constructed jinja2.Environment(autoescape=True) passed via env=. Passing autoescape=True as one of Starlette's **env_options is a deprecated code path (starlette 0.52.1 emits DeprecationWarning) that a future Starlette release can drop entirely. fastapi is unpinned in pyproject.toml, so a routine lockfile upgrade could pull a Starlette version where **env_options passthrough is ignored or removed, defaulting/failing autoescape. The dashboard renders dynamic values (instance names, search history, config values) via {{ }}; silently-disabled autoescaping would turn every template variable into a potential stored/reflected XSS sink. Verified empirically: on pinned starlette 0.52.1 the kwarg still takes effect (deprecation warning only) - not an active vulnerability today, but it removes intentional hardening with no version pin or regression test guarding it.
findings.fix_hint: Restore the preconfigured jinja2.Environment(loader=..., autoescape=True) passed via Jinja2Templates(env=...) as in commit e11187e, and/or add a regression test asserting templates.env.autoescape is True, and/or pin an upper bound on fastapi/starlette.
findings.why_it_matters: Autoescaping is the primary XSS defense for server-rendered HTML. If a dependency upgrade silently disables it, every {{ }} interpolation becomes an XSS vector with no signal until an attacker-controlled string is rendered unescaped.

findings.title: Revert adopts deprecated Starlette env_options API, emitting DeprecationWarning at every startup
findings.problem: This uncommitted diff reverts HEAD commit e11187e, changing Jinja2Templates(env=_jinja_env) (preconfigured jinja2.Environment) back to Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True). Verified against installed Starlette 0.52.1: the **env_options passthrough is NOT removed - it is DEPRECATED. Jinja2Templates.__init__ emits 'DeprecationWarning: Extra environment options are deprecated. Use a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead.' whenever any env_option is passed, and this module is imported at app/scheduler startup, so the warning fires on every process start. The reverted form adopts a framework API on a documented deprecation path, moving AWAY from the vendor-recommended pattern the HEAD commit had just aligned to. This is the sole Jinja2Templates call site in the repo. SECURITY NON-ISSUE noted explicitly: both forms yield templates.env.autoescape == True on Starlette 0.52.1; this revert does NOT disable HTML autoescaping.
findings.fix_hint: Keep the HEAD-commit form (preconfigured jinja2.Environment passed via env=). It is warning-free and forward-compatible. If the revert is intentional for another reason, capture that reason.
findings.why_it_matters: The code moves from the vendor-recommended API back to a deprecated one with no observable functional benefit; a DeprecationWarning fires on every process start.

findings.title: Reverts deliberate autoescape hardening back onto deprecated Jinja2Templates env_options path
findings.problem: This diff reverts commit e11187e which explicitly replaced Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True) with a preconfigured jinja2.Environment. Verified against installed starlette 0.52.1: the concrete __init__ still has **env_options and forwards autoescape=True at runtime (only the public @overload type stubs dropped it), so autoescape is NOT silently broken today - but starlette emits DeprecationWarning on every process start (this call runs at import time). pytest config has no filterwarnings so CI will not fail, but this (a) silently re-introduces the exact deprecated API surface the prior commit deliberately moved off of, and (b) will break outright if a future starlette release removes **env_options from the concrete __init__. Given a working, tested alternative already exists in git history, this looks like an accidental revert (bad merge/rebase or reverting the wrong commit) rather than an intentional simplification.
findings.fix_hint: Restore the preconfigured-Environment pattern from commit e11187e: _jinja_env = jinja2.Environment(loader=jinja2.FileSystemLoader(str(TEMPLATES_DIR)), autoescape=True); templates = Jinja2Templates(env=_jinja_env) (and keep the import jinja2 this diff removed).
findings.why_it_matters: Re-adopting a deprecated framework API that a prior commit deliberately moved off of creates a silent forward-compatibility hazard for the app's only template construction site.

findings.title: Deprecated template env options can break startup when warnings are fatal
findings.problem: Line 45 passes autoescape=True as an extra Jinja2Templates environment option. In the installed Starlette implementation, any env_options trigger a DeprecationWarning before the template environment is created. Verified that PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 .venv/bin/python -W error::DeprecationWarning -c 'import triggarr.web.routes' fails at this line. The previous code used a preconfigured jinja2.Environment, which is the non-deprecated path. The likely impact is an import-time application startup failure in CI, packaging, or production environments that treat deprecations as errors, plus a compatibility hazard when Starlette removes these extra env options.
findings.fix_hint: Do not pass autoescape=True through Jinja2Templates(directory=...). Either revert to a preconfigured jinja2.Environment or call Jinja2Templates(directory=str(TEMPLATES_DIR)) and add a focused test asserting autoescape remains enabled.
findings.why_it_matters: Import-time application startup failure in CI, packaging, or production environments that treat deprecations as errors, plus a compatibility hazard when Starlette removes these extra env options.
